summary: Regression smoke test after adding Vercel build-config files (/app/frontend/.npmrc and /app/frontend/vercel.json). Verified files exist with correct contents; confirmed local app still renders home, shop, and admin login without new runtime errors. Vercel deploy itself cannot be validated from this environment - final validation requires user redeploy on Vercel.
passed: /app/frontend/vercel.json exists with framework='create-react-app', buildCommand='yarn build', installCommand='yarn install --ignore-engines', outputDirectory='build'
Home page (/) loads with hero 'Sacred Beads. Certified Purity.', nav (HOME/SHOP/CATEGORIES/JOURNAL/ABOUT/CONTACT), CTAs (Explore Collection, Read the Journal), announcement bar
Shop page (/shop) loads with 6 products, filters (Collection, Product Category, Mukhi), sort dropdown
Admin login (/admin/login) loads with email/password fields and Sign In button
No new console errors introduced by build-config file additions
action_items: User to redeploy on Vercel to confirm the ERESOLVE peer-dep conflict is resolved by legacy-peer-deps=true + yarn install override

success_rate.backend: N/A (not tested - frontend only per request)

notes: Only pre-existing benign console warning observed (empty string passed to img src attribute) - unrelated to this change. .npmrc and vercel.json are build-time-only configs and do not affect local runtime, as expected.
